text:
There once was a girl named Patricia. She lived on an island surrounded by fog all the time. That's not unusual. Islands do that.

Patricia loved adventure. She'd spend her days collecting bird feathers. She had an enormous collection. She had plans for them. Maybe she would sew hats out of them.

One foggy day, while climbing a tree looking for birds, she found something unusual stuck in one of the feathers. A thing that shouldn't be there. Something shiny.

Curious, she brushed it off. It wasn't scrap metal. It was a tiny key.

She discovered a tiny lock. One that might hold something of importance.

Days passed. Patricia tried the key in every lock she found. Nothing worked.

Then a storm blew in. Rain turned to sleet. Her key collection vanished.

Patricia became despondent.

That night, under the storm, a light opened. She stumbled inside.

What awaited her?

A tiny room, with an old chest
